// comment: The Mono.Cecil 0.11.5 test suite (ported here into CodeBrix.AssemblyTools.Tests)
was designed for NUnit's default sequential-within-fixture execution model.
Many tests round-trip assemblies through shared temp paths under
Path.GetTempPath()/cecil-irt and /cecil-drt via TestRunner.RoundTrip, so running
test collections in parallel races them into IOException ('file is being used
by another process') and into library-internal non-thread-safe collection writes.
Disabling cross-collection parallelism restores the upstream execution model.
Individual test methods inside a collection still run sequentially. ~11-second
total wall-clock time is unaffected because the suite isn't CPU-bound.
